text.title: Kubernetes Cluster Backup with Velero Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the backup-kubernetes-velero methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale and is referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Velero installations MUST use the Kopia data mover (default in 1.15+); the Restic uploader is deprecated and MUST NOT be enabled for new clusters.

rationale.source: Velero docs — Data Mover, Velero v1.15 release notes

rule.rationale: Restic uploader is marked deprecated; new install paths default to Kopia which is faster and uses native CSI snapshots.

rule.statement: The cluster MUST have a CSI driver with VolumeSnapshotClass installed; Velero MUST use CSI snapshots (`--features=EnableCSI`) rather than restic-file-copy fallback when available.
rule.rationale: File-copy fallback is order-of-magnitude slower and loses application-consistent snapshot semantics.

rule.statement: Schedule CRDs targeting stateful workloads (Postgres, MySQL, Mongo) MUST declare `pre.hook.backup.velero.io/command` (quiesce / fsfreeze) and `post.hook.backup.velero.io/command` (unfreeze).
rule.rationale: Without quiesce, the snapshot is crash-consistent only; application data files may be torn mid-write.

rule.statement: BackupStorageLocation MUST point to an object store in a DIFFERENT cloud or region than the cluster's control plane.
rule.rationale: Co-located backups die with the cluster in a regional outage; cross-region/cluster is the only resilient pattern.

rule.statement: Every Schedule CRD MUST set `ttl` explicitly (no infinite retention); recommended ttl: daily=168h, weekly=720h, monthly=2160h.
rule.rationale: Unbounded retention grows object-store cost linearly without RTO benefit.

rule.statement: If all in-cluster state is reconciled from Git + external object storage (pure GitOps, no PVC state), this methodology MUST be skipped.
rule.rationale: Backing up derived state duplicates the source of truth; reconcile-from-Git is faster restore than Velero.
